summary: The reproduction demonstrates that the isLocalhost() function in esm.sh fails to block IPv6 localhost addresses (::1) and other private IP ranges, allowing SSRF attacks via the /http://[::1]:port/ endpoint.
expected_security_impact: The test program should demonstrate that isLocalhost() returns false for IPv6 localhost (::1) and other private IPs, proving that SSRF protection can be bypassed. An internal service on [::1]:8080 should be accessible via the bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and executed the test program which demonstrated: 1) isLocalhost('::1') returns false (should be true), 2) isLocalhost('[::1]') returns false (should be true), 3) isLocalhost('10.0.0.1') returns false (should be true for private IPs), 4) The test program successfully connected to an internal service at http://[::1]:8080/secret, confirming SSRF.
verification_method: The reproduction extracts the exact isLocalhost() function from server/utils.go and tests it with various hostnames. It then starts an internal HTTP service on [::1]:8080 and demonstrates that a client can connect to it using the IPv6 localhost address that bypasses the isLocalhost() check.
evidence_produced_by_run: isLocalhost('::1') = false (VULNERABILITY: should be true for IPv6 localhost)
isLocalhost('[::1]') = false (VULNERABILITY: should be true for IPv6 localhost)
isLocalhost('10.0.0.1') = false (VULNERABILITY: should be true for private IP)
isLocalhost('172.16.0.1') = false (VULNERABILITY: should be true for private IP)
HTTP request to http://[::1]:8080/secret succeeded, returning internal service content
SSRF protection bypass confirmed for IPv6 localhost address

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the pinned commit (main), and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
